This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering several health benefits, including its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Robinsonella pleiopoda include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
